prompt_text:
Analyze the following java source code repository for violations of the Open/Closed Principle (OCP).

## Principle Definition
Software entities should be open for extension but closed for modification. When adding new behavior requires modifying existing code (e.g., adding elif branches, modifying switch statements), it violates OCP.

## What to Look For
- Long if/elif/else or switch chains that would need modification to add new types
- Functions with type-checking logic (isinstance checks) to ...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/config/ir/compiler/EventCondition.java",
      "entity_name": "EventCondition.Compiler.buildCondition",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 100,
      "line_end": 125,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Type-checking logic using instanceof chain for expression compilation",
      "reasoning": "The buildCondition method uses a long chain of instanceof checks to determine how to compile different BooleanExpression types. Adding a new expression type to the Logstash configuration language requires modifying this central compiler method instead of extending the system through polymorphism or a visitor pattern."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/lib/logstash/settings.rb",
      "entity_name": "LogStash::Config::DSL.validate_value",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 360,
      "line_end": 500,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Monolithic case statement for configuration value validation",
      "reasoning": "The validate_value method contains a massive case statement that handles every supported validator type (string, number, boolean, password, etc.). Adding a new data type or validation logic to Logstash settings requires modifying this core method, violating the principle that the system should be open for extension but closed for modification."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/execution/AbstractPipelineExt.java",
      "entity_name": "AbstractPipelineExt.getPipelineSourceDetails",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 650,
      "line_end": 670,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Switch statement on protocol strings for source details",
      "reasoning": "This method uses a switch statement to handle different configuration source protocols ('string', 'file', 'module', etc.). Adding a new source type requires modifying this execution class. This logic should be delegated to the source objects themselves."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "logstash-core/src/main/java/org/logstash/FieldReference.java",
      "entity_name": "FieldReference.setEscapeStyle",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 50,
      "line_end": 65,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Hardcoded switch
